records.title: Gross pay starts each pay period
records.intent: A fictional payroll run starts from explicit gross pay for the current pay period.
records.given: A pay_period_started event declares periodIndex, gross, periodsPerYear, filingStatus, and allowances.
records.when: getPaycheck and getYearToDate are called before deductions, benefits, bonuses, taxes, credits, or garnishments.
records.then: gross and regularGross equal the event gross amount, bonusGross is zero, and YTD gross starts from current gross. Worked example: gross 2000.00, 26 periods, single filing, 0 allowances. Expected values: paycheck.gross 2000.00, paycheck.regularGross 2000.00, paycheck.bonusGross 0.00, ytd.gross 2000.00.

records.title: Pre-tax deductions reduce both taxable bases
records.intent: Employee pre-tax deductions reduce federal-style and state-style taxable bases before withholding is computed.
records.given: A pay period has one or more pre_tax_deduction_set events keyed by code.
records.when: getPaycheck is called after the pre-tax deductions are set.
records.then: preTaxTotal is the sum of pre-tax deductions, both taxable bases equal gross minus preTaxTotal, and the pre_tax line item reports the same total. Worked example: gross 2000.00 plus retirement pre-tax deduction 100.00. Expected values: paycheck.preTaxTotal 100.00, paycheck.taxableBaseFederal 1900.00, paycheck.taxableBaseState 1900.00, lineItems.pre_tax 100.00.

records.title: Federalish withholding annualizes taxable pay and applies brackets
records.intent: Federalish withholding annualizes regular taxable federal wages, taxes them through fictional brackets, and de-annualizes to the period.
records.given: The employee is single and has no allowances; taxable federal wages are gross minus pre-tax deductions.
records.then: single brackets are 10 percent up to 12000 annual, 20 percent to 48000 annual, and 30 percent above, divided back by periodsPerYear. Worked example: gross 2000.00, pre-tax 100.00, 26 periods, single, 0 allowances. Expected values: paycheck.withholding.federalish 339.23.

records.title: Filing status selects the bracket table
records.intent: The payroll engine supports single and joint federalish bracket tables. Filing status changes only the federalish bracket table.
records.given: A pay_period_started event declares filingStatus as joint.
records.when: getPaycheck is called for the same taxable base as the single example.
records.then: joint brackets are 8 percent up to 24000 annual, 18 percent to 96000 annual, and 28 percent above, divided back by periodsPerYear. Worked example: gross 2000.00, pre-tax 100.00, 26 periods, joint, 0 allowances. Expected values: paycheck.withholding.federalish 249.69.

records.title: Allowances reduce the annual federalish base before bracketing
records.intent: Each allowance reduces annualized regular taxable federal base by 4000.00 before brackets are applied, floored at zero.
records.given: The pay period declares allowances on pay_period_started.
records.then: federalish uses max(0, taxableBaseFederal times periodsPerYear minus allowances times 4000.00), then de-annualizes bracket tax. Worked example: gross 2000.00, pre-tax 100.00, 26 periods, single, 2 allowances. Expected values: paycheck.withholding.federalish 272.31.

records.title: Stateish withholding uses a flat rate on its own base
records.intent: Stateish withholding is a fictional flat 5 percent of taxableBaseState. At this point taxableBaseState is gross minus ordinary pre-tax deductions.
records.given: A pay period has gross and ordinary pre-tax deductions.
records.then: stateish equals taxableBaseState times 5 percent and is reported separately from federalish. Worked example: gross 2000.00 with pre-tax deduction 100.00. Expected values: paycheck.taxableBaseState 1900.00, paycheck.withholding.stateish 95.00.

records.title: Socialish payroll tax stops at a YTD wage-base cap
records.intent: Socialish tax is 6 percent of current gross only until socialish wages reach the fictional 50000.00 annual wage base.
records.given: A prior_ytd_set event supplies prior socialishWages and the current period has gross pay.
records.then: only current gross under the remaining wage base is taxed at 6 percent, while YTD socialishWages still includes all current gross. Worked example: prior socialishWages 49000.00 and current gross 2000.00. Expected values: paycheck.withholding.socialish 60.00, ytd.socialishWages 51000.00, ytd.socialishTax 60.00.

records.title: Medicareish tax adds a YTD surtax above threshold
records.intent: Medicareish tax is 1.5 percent of all current gross, plus an extra 0.9 percent only on current-period wages that cross 30000.00 YTD.
records.given: A prior_ytd_set event supplies prior medicareishWages and current gross crosses the threshold.
records.then: the surtax is computed only on newly-crossed wages, not all prior YTD wages. Worked example: prior medicareishWages 29500.00 and current gross 1000.00. Expected values: paycheck.withholding.medicareish 19.50, ytd.medicareishWages 30500.00, ytd.medicareishTax 19.50.

records.title: Refundable credit phases out as annualized gross rises
records.intent: A fictional refundable payroll credit starts at 40.00 per period and phases out by 5 percent of annualized gross above 40000.00.
records.given: A pay period has current regular gross and no bonus.
records.then: creditTotal equals max(0, 40.00 minus max(0, annualizedGross minus 40000.00) times 5 percent divided by periodsPerYear). Worked example: current regular gross 2000.00 over 26 periods. Expected values: paycheck.creditTotal 16.92.

records.title: Post-tax deductions reduce net after withholding and credits
records.intent: Post-tax deductions are withheld from net pay after taxes and refundable credits; they do not reduce taxable bases.
records.given: A post_tax_deduction_set event declares a current-period deduction amount.
records.then: postTaxTotal sums post-tax deductions, lineItems includes post_tax, and net is reduced after withholding and credits. Worked example: gross 1200.00 with post-tax deduction 75.00. Expected values: paycheck.postTaxTotal 75.00, lineItems.post_tax 75.00, paycheck.net 821.15.

records.title: Benefit elections are pre-tax federal but post-tax state
records.intent: A benefit_elected amount reduces taxableBaseFederal and preTaxTotal, but does not reduce taxableBaseState.
records.given: The employee has an ordinary pre-tax deduction and a benefit election.
records.then: preTaxTotal includes ordinary pre-tax deductions and benefits; taxableBaseFederal subtracts both, while taxableBaseState subtracts only ordinary pre-tax deductions. Worked example: gross 2000.00, ordinary pre-tax 100.00, benefit 50.00. Expected values: paycheck.preTaxTotal 150.00, paycheck.taxableBaseFederal 1850.00, paycheck.taxableBaseState 1900.00, paycheck.withholding.stateish 95.00, lineItems.benefit 50.00.

records.title: Bonuses use supplemental withholding and count toward YTD wages
records.intent: bonus_paid adds current bonus gross. Bonus dollars are withheld at 22 percent supplemental federalish rate and still count in YTD wages.
records.given: A pay period includes regular gross and one bonus_paid event.
records.then: bonusGross equals bonus amount, gross includes regular plus bonus, federalish includes 22 percent of bonusGross, and YTD wages include the bonus. Worked example: regular gross 1500.00 with bonus 500.00. Expected values: paycheck.bonusGross 500.00, paycheck.gross 2000.00, paycheck.withholding.federalish 363.85, ytd.gross 2000.00, ytd.socialishWages 2000.00.

records.title: Single garnishment is capped by disposable earnings
records.intent: A garnishment order is applied after withholding and credits. It is limited by requested amount, per-order percent cap, and the shared combined cap.
records.given: One garnishment_order_set event exists for the current period.
records.then: disposable earnings are gross minus pre-tax totals and withholding plus credits, before post-tax deductions. Worked example: gross 1200.00 with one order amount 400.00 and percentCap 15. Expected values: paycheck.garnishmentTotal 134.42, lineItems.garnishment 134.42, paycheck.net 761.73.

records.title: Priority garnishments share a combined cap
records.intent: Multiple garnishments are processed by ascending priority. Each order has its own percent cap, and all orders together cannot exceed 25 percent of disposable earnings.
records.given: Two garnishment_order_set events exist with different priorities.
records.then: the lower priority number is filled first up to its own cap, then later orders receive only the remaining combined cap. Worked example: gross 1200.00 with priority 1 amount 400.00 cap 20 and priority 2 amount 300.00 cap 20. Expected values: paycheck.garnishmentTotal 224.04.

records.title: Additional flat withholding reduces net but not garnishment base
records.intent: Employee-elected additional withholding reduces net pay after ordinary withholding and credits, but disposable earnings for garnishment are defined before additional withholding.
records.given: An additional_withholding_set event declares a current-period amount alongside a garnishment order.
records.then: withholding.additional equals the declared amount, net is reduced by that amount, and garnishmentTotal remains based on disposable earnings before additional withholding. Worked example: gross 1200.00, additional withholding 25.00, one 15 percent garnishment. Expected values: paycheck.withholding.additional 25.00, paycheck.garnishmentTotal 134.42, paycheck.net 736.73, lineItems.additional 25.00.

records.title: Bonus gross recomputes the credit phase-out
records.intent: The refundable credit phase-out uses current regular gross plus current bonus gross. Adding a bonus can reduce a credit that regular gross alone would receive.
records.given: A pay period has regular gross and a bonus_paid event.
records.then: annualizedGross for the credit equals regularGross plus bonusGross times periodsPerYear, so bonusGross is included in the phase-out. Worked example: regular gross 1500.00 with bonus 500.00. Expected values: paycheck.creditTotal 16.92.

records.title: Components are rounded once and net is the residual
records.intent: Exposed paycheck components are rounded half-up to cents at reporting stage. Net is computed from full-precision intermediate values and then rounded once.
records.given: A period uses fractional gross, deductions, and rates that create fractions of a cent.
records.then: reported component values have cent precision, and net is the one-time rounded residual after all full-precision components are applied. Worked example: gross 1234.56, pre-tax 123.45, benefit 67.89, post-tax 12.34, additional 7.89. Expected values: paycheck.withholding.federalish 131.72, paycheck.withholding.stateish 55.56, paycheck.withholding.socialish 74.07, paycheck.withholding.medicareish 18.52, paycheck.creditTotal 40.00, paycheck.net 783.12; paycheck.net rounded to 2 decimals.

records.title: Duplicate events are idempotent and bonus voids undo bonus effects
records.intent: Events carry ids for idempotency. Replaying an event with the same id is ignored. A bonus_voided event removes the matching bonus and recomputes all derived values.
records.given: The same bonus_paid event id is applied twice and then a bonus_voided event references that bonusId.
records.then: the duplicate event does not double the bonus, the void removes it, and federalish, credit, payroll taxes, net, and YTD values are recomputed without the bonus. Worked example: regular gross 1500.00, a 500.00 bonus replayed twice, then bonus_voided for that bonusId. Expected values: paycheck.bonusGross 0.00, paycheck.gross 1500.00, paycheck.withholding.federalish 253.85, paycheck.creditTotal 40.00, paycheck.withholding.socialish 90.00, paycheck.withholding.medicareish 22.50, paycheck.net 1098.65, ytd.gross 1500.00, ytd.socialishWages 1500.00.
